dominant_palette: warm gold, white/cream, deep black, cool silver, crimson red
secondary_palette: emerald green, cobalt blue, tartan plaid green-navy
composition_vocabulary: Tiles split between clean studio product shots on white or neutral grounds and dense editorial/typographic compositions with heavy text overlay; the jewellery half favours tight centred framing with abundant negative space, while the lower half collapses into flat, busy graphic design and lifestyle photography.
lighting_quality: Mixed — upper tiles use soft, directional studio light that polishes metal and lifts diamonds; lower tiles shift to flat ambient or screen-glow light, with no consistent chiaroscuro logic across the set.
material_textures: polished gold, brilliant-cut diamond, liquid mercury / molten metal, brushed silver, woven fabric (tartan), matte black cap fabric
aesthetic_register: The sample straddles luxury heirloom jewellery and contemporary streetwear-adjacent graphic culture — fine high-jewellery references share the board with brand identity mockups, event posters, and drop-culture merchandise, suggesting a deliberate high-low collision.
notably_absent: Naturalistic or organic imagery — botanical elements, stone settings in earthy tones, hand-modelled lifestyle contexts, and the softly lit 'quiet luxury' aesthetic common on jewellery moodboards are almost entirely missing.
